name: DuPont de Nemours, Inc.
summary: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. is a leading provider of advanced solutions across healthcare, water, construction, and industrial markets. Its two segments—Healthcare & Water Technologies and Diversified Industrials—offer high-performance medical packaging, medical device components, water filtration and purification technologies, building technologies for residential and non-residential construction, and industrial adhesive, wear, friction, and packaging solutions serving aerospace, automotive, and printing markets.

products.name: Medical Packaging Solutions
products.description: High-performance packaging for medical device and biopharma markets.

products.name: Water Filtration and Purification Technologies
products.description: Industrial wastewater, municipal drinking water, desalination, and life sciences filtration solutions.

products.name: Specialty Medical Devices and Components
products.description: Advanced manufactured components for structural heart, electrophysiology, surgical robotics, and cardiovascular applications.

products.name: Building Technologies
products.description: Broad portfolio for new-build and repair/remodel applications in non-residential and residential construction.

products.name: Industrial Adhesive, Wear and Friction Solutions
products.description: Adhesive, wear, friction, and packaging solutions for aerospace, automotive, and printing markets.

products.name: Protective Garments
products.description: High-performance protective garments for industrial and safety applications.

partnerships.description: Divested Nutrition & Biosciences business to IFF via Reverse Morris Trust transaction in 2021.
Divested majority of Materials & Mobility segment businesses to Celanese in 2022.
Divested Delrin business to TJC in 2023; agreed to sell Aramids business to Arclin, a TJC affiliate portfolio company, in 2025.
Arrangement to share future eligible PFAS costs among DuPont, Corteva, and Chemours.
Arrangement to share future eligible PFAS costs among DuPont, Corteva, and Chemours.
